_comment: Registry for the `design:` frontmatter back-reference â€” the planâ†’designâ†’blog traceability chain. Each blog post may declare `design: <slug>`; this file is the contract that says which slugs are valid and which design doc each one points at. The design docs live in a SIBLING repo (earlbear-agentic-workflow), so this committed registry is what the traceability check validates against â€” it works even when that repo is not checked out. Layers: (1) the zod schema validates the slug SHAPE at build; (2) check-design-traceability.py BLOCKS a post whose `design:` slug is not a key below; (3) the same check, with --check-docs, ADVISORILY verifies each `doc` path resolves to a real file when the sibling repo is present. Keep this in sync with the sibling design docs; a slug whose doc is renamed/removed there should be updated here. Origin: commit d7e50b1 'Add design: back-references to design-capturing posts'.
